extraction.one_line_summary: FORMOLO David soloed from Col de la Madeleine descent through final 14.8km climb, holding 33 seconds over surging ROGLIC
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma four-rider pacemaking systematically shattered Ineos morale before teammates could contribute, reversing traditional Grand Tour dynamics.
FORMOLO attacked on Madeleine descent not summit, gambling on technical advantage over pure climbing to build 4:19 buffer before finale.
ROGLIC delayed final acceleration until sub-100m, prioritizing bonus seconds over stage win when gap to breakaway was manageable.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: dropped immediately on final climb without doing any work, pre-Tour leadership battle with Bernal effectively resolved
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: dropped with 12km remaining alongside domestiques, Castroviejo escorted him suggesting protective team strategy not fitness crisis
rider_signals.SIVAKOV Pavel: negative: dropped before contributing any pacemaking effort, Ineos depth unexpectedly shallow
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: ex-mountain biker delivered final pacing shift before Roglic, signaling Jumbo's superior climbing reserve compared to Ineos
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: dropped early but commentary suggests deliberate conservation for upcoming stages, not competitive collapse
rider_signals.FORMOLO David: positive: descended Madeleine recklessly fast despite multiple scares, tactical risk-taking paid off with 4+ minute buffer
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: accelerated sub-100m only for bonus seconds despite having firepower to chase stage, coldly pragmatic GC racing
